title: Valid Sudoku Solver Backtracking Bug Fix

statement.markdown: Solve the problem and fix the bugs.
statement.inputFormat: The input will be a 1D array of 81 integers representing a 9x9 Sudoku grid.
statement.outputFormat: The output should be a boolean indicating whether the Sudoku grid is valid according to Sudoku rules.

hints.text: Check if the current cell's number is already present in the current row, column or 3x3 box.

editorial.title: Valid Sudoku Solver Backtracking Editorials
editorial.intuition: The backtracking algorithm tries all possible values for each cell from 1 to 9.
editorial.bruteForce: The brute force approach tries all possible values for each cell from 1 to 9.
editorial.betterSolution: Use a set or hash map to keep track of the values in the current row, column and 3x3 box.
editorial.optimalSolution: Use a set or hash map to keep track of the values in the current row, column and 3x3 box.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The backtracking algorithm will find the first valid assignment for all cells.
commonMistakes: Not checking if the current cell's number is already present in the current row, column or 3x3 box.
